_note: This file's 'hooks' section is intentionally empty. Claude Code loads plugin-level hooks.json directly, so putting unresolved path placeholders here would create failing hooks alongside the correctly configured ones in settings.json. The cold-start interview writes the real commands — with the resolved billing data path — into the user's settings.json instead.

UserPromptSubmit.description: Writes a Unix timestamp on the first user message of each session. Used by billing-stop.ps1 to compute elapsed time. Deleting this file (done by billing-status on log or skip) resets the timer.

Stop.description: Blocks session close when billing is enabled, a per-session timer file exists at .sessions/[attorney-slug]_[session-id], and a legal matter is active. Outputs {"decision":"block","reason":"..."} (including the timer file path) to prompt Claude to run /billing:billing-status --session-end.
